evidence: `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/handleError.ts` combines normalization/logging with optional toast side effects (`notifyError`), then is consumed by both infrastructure (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`) and feature hooks (`src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMediaGalleryHandlers.ts`, `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts`).
`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` allows runtime fallback initialization in `getSupabaseClient()` after a failed access path, so any importer can implicitly bootstrap client state.

`src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx` acts as a high-fan-in orchestration hub joining auth, user settings, selection, CRUD, defaults, and a non-React runtime snapshot side effect (`setProjectSelectionSnapshot`).

Interaction behavior is implemented via bespoke local policies in multiple places: tooltip click/hover toggling state machine in `src/pages/Home/components/panes/CreativePartnerPane.tsx`, global listener lifecycle in `src/shared/components/MediaGallery/hooks/useMobileInteractions.ts`, and controlled-dialog event cancellation strategy in `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/LightboxShell.tsx`.

The shared UI layer mixes primitives, themed wrappers, and pass-through barrels (`src/shared/components/ui/tooltip.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/button.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/label.tsx`, `src/shared/components/ui/cn.ts`, `src/shared/components/ui/sonner.tsx`) which blurs ownership boundaries of what is truly foundational vs app-specific.

`src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/ShotActions.tsx` and `src/shared/components/MediaGalleryItem/components/InfoTooltip.tsx` each instantiate tooltip providers/content behavior independently, reinforcing local ad-hoc interaction conventions over a single composition pattern.

Join-clips compat keeps a mixed canonical+legacy parameter bag (`JoinClipsTaskCompatParams`) and then strips/normalizes it into canonical shape, creating an extra abstraction layer with little leverage.
Canonical join-clips export is itself a pass-through wrapper (`createCanonicalJoinClipsTask` just returns `createJoinClipsTaskInternal`), adding interface surface without additional behavior.

`useInlineEditState` imports a large set of hooks/types/utils directly from `shared/components/MediaLightbox/*`, indicating feature-level dependency on another feature's internals rather than a stable shared boundary.
`InlineEditView` composes MediaLightbox components and provider contracts directly, so tool-specific edit flow is tightly coupled to MediaLightbox implementation details.

Generation-task scope APIs compute rich status (`ok`, `scope_mismatch`, `missing_project_scope`, `query_failed`) in `generationTaskRepository.ts`, but downstream seams collapse or ignore those distinctions.
`useLineageChain.ts` converts any non-`ok` scope result into a generic thrown error (`Variant scope validation failed (...)`), reducing actionable context at the UI seam.

`generationTaskCache.ts` caches non-`ok` mapping statuses but only explicitly handles `query_failed`; other invalid states (like `scope_mismatch`) become silent `taskId: null` cache outcomes without explicit seam behavior.

`src/shared/hooks/useDeleteGenerationWithConfirm.tsx` exits early when `selectedProjectId` is missing but leaves `pendingDeleteId` unchanged, so dialog state can remain stale/open and require extra UI interaction to recover.

Deprecation signaling behavior is inconsistent: `src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts` and `src/shared/components/theme/buttonVariants.ts` call warning logic at import time, while other shims (for example `src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts`, `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/reportRuntimeError.ts`) warn at function invocation.
Import-time warning side effects reduce local predictability and make behavior dependent on module loading rather than actual deprecated API usage.

Edge helpers expose multiple incompatible failure contracts in the same API surface: `initEdgeRuntime` returns `{ runtime?, errorResponse? }` while `ensureRequestMethod`/`parseJsonBody` return `OperationResult`, and `requestMethodFailureResponse`/`operationFailureResponse` convert failures into `Response` envelopes.

Service-layer APIs handling similar operational failures use different public contracts: `generateVideo` and `consumePendingJoinClips` return `OperationResult`, while `fetchToolSettingsSupabase` throws `ToolSettingsError` and `useLineageChain` path throws through React Query.

`truncateText` in `stringFormatting.ts` violates max-length semantics by returning `text.slice(0, maxLength) + '...'` (result length exceeds `maxLength`), while sibling formatter `cropFilename` keeps output within max-length budgeting.

Deprecated alias modules are still active at scale: `src/shared/lib/toolConstants.ts` is imported in 65 files and `src/shared/lib/errorHandling/reportRuntimeError.ts` is referenced in 36 files, so migration to canonical entrypoints is still incomplete.

`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/legacyStructureVideo.ts` keeps deprecated `structure_video_*` and `use_uni3c` fields as part of active task param contracts, while `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` still accepts multiple legacy input shapes.

Only some compat paths enforce removal date behavior (`joinClipsCompatAdapter` uses `isPastRemovalTarget`), while others remain passive type-level shims (`legacyStructureVideo`), creating uneven migration pressure.

holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles shows src/shared/hooks/ has 81 production files and src/shared/lib/ has 72 production files, both above the >10 flat-directory overload signal for mixed concerns.

src/shared/lib/dragDrop.ts duplicates core helpers already present in src/shared/lib/dragDropCore.ts (DataTransfer type detection) and src/shared/lib/dragDropPreview.ts (createDragPreview), but only dragDrop.ts is imported by active callers.

review_context.existing_findings marks src/shared/lib/dragDropCore.ts as orphaned with zero importers and src/shared/lib/dragDropPreview.ts as untested with zero importers, confirming structural dead placement rather than active modularization.

`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useSharedLightboxState.ts` defines a very wide composed input contract (`UseSharedLightboxStateProps`, lines 126-137) and a very wide return model (`UseSharedLightboxStateReturn`, lines 143-252), then acts as a central composition hub (lines 626-730).

`src/tools/travel-between-images/components/Timeline/hooks/useTimelineOrchestrator.ts` combines timeline dimensions, drag, zoom, selection, drop, duplicate collision handling, endpoint dragging, and structure-video placement in one hook (lines 163-569) and returns a very large surface (lines 570-655).

`supabase/functions/complete_task/index.ts` performs request parsing, auth, rate limiting, storage, param mutation, generation creation, orchestrator follow-up, and billing follow-up in one linear handler (lines 131-430), while related schema fallback behavior is also encoded in `params.ts` and `generation-child.ts`.

findings.summary: A single error facade couples infrastructure and UI side effects, making error policy a cross-layer dependency hub.
evidence: `handleError` both normalizes/logs and conditionally triggers toast notifications (`notifyError`), not just reporting.
Supabase runtime client initialization paths call `handleError`/`handleAndRethrow`, so infra code imports UI-capable error behavior.
Feature hooks also call the same facade (`reportError` / `reportAndRethrow` / `handleError`), concentrating many module boundaries behind one side-effectful contract.
findings.suggestion: Split the API into a pure cross-layer reporter (normalize/log/typed return) and a UI notifier adapter used only at presentation boundaries; keep infra modules on the pure contract and inject notification policy from UI composition roots.

findings.summary: Supabase client access performs implicit fallback initialization, weakening startup boundary guarantees.
evidence: `getSupabaseClient()` catches runtime access errors and calls `initializeSupabase()` as fallback, then warns once in dev.
This pattern allows arbitrary downstream modules to trigger integration bootstrapping indirectly instead of enforcing explicit bootstrap order.
Large orchestrator providers like `ProjectContext` increase the chance of implicit dependency timing problems because multiple subsystems initialize during render lifecycle.
findings.suggestion: Make bootstrap explicit and fail-fast: remove lazy fallback from `getSupabaseClient`, require `initializeSupabase` in app startup, and expose a narrow readiness contract (or provider) to consumers that need guaranteed initialization.

findings.summary: Interaction-close/open behavior is fragmented across components with custom event-state machines instead of a unified interaction policy.
evidence: `CreativePartnerPane` manually coordinates tooltip open/disabled state across click, pointer leave, and mobile behavior.
`useMobileInteractions` adds global `window` and `document` listeners to close UI state and relies on local marker conventions (`[data-popup]`).
`LightboxShell` cancels Base UI open-change events and delegates closing to custom handlers, indicating framework-level dialog behavior has been bypassed by local policy.
findings.suggestion: Define a shared interaction policy layer (hook/util) for overlay dismissal, mobile tap semantics, and tooltip gating; migrate pane/gallery/lightbox consumers onto that policy to reduce bespoke event logic.

findings.summary: The shared UI package mixes primitives, app-themed wrappers, and pass-through exports, obscuring stable ownership boundaries.
evidence: `tooltip.tsx` is a pure re-export facade, while `button.tsx` is a themed composite wrapper, and `cn.ts` is a utility helper under the same UI namespace.
`label.tsx` and `button.tsx` define variant systems with different abstraction levels, while `sonner.tsx` introduces global toast provider/runtime behavior in the same directory.
Consumers across unrelated features import these files as if they are equivalent-level primitives, but the actual responsibilities vary from utility to global runtime UI service.
findings.suggestion: Partition UI into explicit tiers (primitive, themed component, runtime service, utility) with distinct entry points and import rules so feature modules depend on intentional boundaries rather than a mixed shared bucket.

findings.summary: Join-clips compatibility adapter maintains a duplicate mixed-shape API that adds indirection
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskCompatParams` includes both canonical fields (`mode`, `clip_source`, `video_edit`) and legacy fields (`starting_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_video_*`) in one interface.

`createJoinClipsTaskCompat` normalizes this mixed bag and forwards to canonical creation; canonical export itself is a pass-through wrapper (`createCanonicalJoinClipsTask` returns internal function result directly).
findings.suggestion: Narrow compat input to legacy-only fields plus shared core fields, keep canonical-only API in `joinClips.ts`, and remove pass-through async wrappers that do not add validation/translation.

findings.summary: Video lightbox render hook mixes UI rendering and orchestration, obscuring handoff seams
evidence: `useVideoLightboxRenderModel` imports UI components (`VideoEditPanel`, `InfoPanel`) and returns JSX from inside a hook while also constructing state models.

The hook’s panel memo depends on a very large dependency list and `VideoLightbox` consumes a large render-model object (`layoutProps`, `controlsPanelContent`), making boundary behavior difficult to reason about.
findings.suggestion: Split into a pure state/view-model hook and separate presentational panel components; pass a minimal typed view model to `VideoLightbox` rather than returning JSX from hook internals.

findings.summary: Edit-images tool is tightly coupled to MediaLightbox internals across hooks, types, and UI
evidence: `useInlineEditState` imports many MediaLightbox internals (`hooks`, `ImageEditContext` types, `utils`) instead of a narrow shared editing API.
`InlineEditView` directly reuses MediaLightbox components/providers and expects state shaped for those components, increasing change blast radius across features.
findings.suggestion: Extract shared editing primitives into a dedicated feature-neutral module (state contracts, editing hooks, shared UI primitives) and keep MediaLightbox-specific assembly behind its own facade.

findings.summary: Segment slot mode uses an oversized seam that bundles unrelated concerns together
evidence: `UseSegmentSlotModeProps` combines routing/deeplink behavior, structure-video CRUD callbacks, frame updates, and UI navigation in one boundary contract.
`VideoLightbox` only needs a subset of segment-slot behavior (`segmentSlotMode`, nav/index/readOnly flow), but receives a larger object shape from upstream orchestration.
findings.suggestion: Split segment-slot contracts into focused interfaces (navigation state, structure-video operations, mutation actions) and pass only per-consumer slices rather than one omnibus object.

findings.summary: Edge handlers carry too many local responsibilities in single control-flow paths
evidence: `calculate-task-cost/index.ts` includes request validation/auth, task shape parsing, pricing math, idempotency checks, and ledger writes in one module flow.
`complete_task/orchestrator.ts` includes segment completion gating, billing outcome policy decisions, reconciliation persistence, and fallback error marking in one orchestration file.
findings.suggestion: Refactor both flows into staged pipelines with small pure helpers and explicit boundaries (`auth/request`, `lookup/normalize`, `policy`, `persistence`, `response`) to reduce local branching density.

findings.summary: Edge request helpers require callers to juggle three different failure protocols.
evidence: `initEdgeRuntime` returns `{ runtime?, errorResponse? }` (object-union style), `ensureRequestMethod`/`parseJsonBody` return `OperationResult`, and helper functions separately produce `Response` objects.

`checkRateLimit` also returns `OperationResult`, but even hard failures are encoded as `operationSuccess(fallback)` with `degraded` metadata, adding a fourth interpretation layer (`ok=true` does not mean healthy).
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical edge-guard contract (for example `EdgeGuardResult<T> = { ok: true; value: T } | { ok: false; response: Response; code: string }`) and migrate `initEdgeRuntime`, request validation, JSON parsing, and rate-limit checks to that single shape before response emission.

findings.summary: Sibling service modules expose inconsistent failure semantics for similar operations.
evidence: `generateVideo` and `consumePendingJoinClips` return `OperationResult` with `.ok` branching.
`fetchToolSettingsSupabase` throws `ToolSettingsError` on operational failures instead of returning a result object.
`useLineageChain` path throws inside query functions and exposes errors via React Query result state, creating a third failure style in adjacent service/hook seams.
findings.suggestion: Standardize async service boundaries to one failure contract (either explicit `OperationResult` everywhere or throw-only + typed wrappers), then add thin adapters at hook/UI boundaries so callers never need mixed `try/catch` and `.ok` handling in the same workflow.

findings.summary: Rich mapping/scope status is computed upstream but degraded into generic behavior downstream.
evidence: `generationTaskRepository` defines explicit status taxonomy (`scope_mismatch`, `missing_project_scope`, `query_failed`, etc.).
`generationTaskCache` stores status but only special-cases `query_failed`; other non-ok states become silent null mappings.
`useLineageChain` throws generic scope-validation errors for any non-`ok` status, discarding structured cause details at boundary handoff.
findings.suggestion: Promote status taxonomy into a shared discriminated union consumed consistently by cache and hook layers, with explicit handling paths per status (user-actionable mismatch, missing scope, retriable query failure) instead of generic null/error collapse.

findings.summary: `truncateText` exceeds its declared max length while sibling formatter preserves max budget.
evidence: `truncateText(text, maxLength)` returns ``${text.slice(0, maxLength)}...```, producing output length `maxLength + 3` when truncated.
In the same module, `cropFilename` performs length-aware ellipsis budgeting to keep output constrained, so two neighboring formatters expose conflicting max-length semantics.
findings.suggestion: Align formatter contracts by making `truncateText` reserve ellipsis space (`slice(0, maxLength - 3) + '...'`) or rename it to explicitly signal overrun behavior; then add unit tests documenting expected output lengths.

findings.summary: Deprecated alias surfaces are still heavily used as live entrypoints
evidence: `toolConstants.ts` is explicitly marked deprecated but remains imported broadly (65 import sites), including direct use from `editVideoDefaults.ts`.
`reportRuntimeError.ts` is deprecated but still referenced broadly (36 files), so migration to `handleError` has not completed.
This keeps compatibility wrappers as operational APIs rather than temporary boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Execute a staged codemod migration: replace `TOOL_IDS/TOOL_ROUTES` imports with `toolIds/toolRoutes` and replace `reportRuntimeError` call sites with `handleError`; then keep shim files as thin re-exports for one release and finally remove them with CI enforcement on forbidden import paths.

findings.summary: Deprecation warnings run at module import time in some shims
evidence: `toolConstants.ts` executes `warnIfPastRemovalTarget()` at top level.
`buttonVariants.ts` also executes `warnIfPastRemovalTarget()` at top level.
Other shims in this batch (`useCredits.ts`) warn only when deprecated functions are invoked, creating inconsistent behavior and noisy side effects tied to import order.
findings.suggestion: Move warning calls behind exported function/property access (or a dedicated explicit `assertDeprecatedImportUsed()` hook) so deprecation telemetry reflects real usage instead of import side effects.

findings.summary: Travel/join-clips task creation still maintains broad dual legacy+canonical schema
evidence: `joinClipsCompatAdapter.ts` accepts multiple legacy field families (`starting_video_path`, `video_edit_mode`, `source_*`) and converts them to canonical shape.
`legacyStructureVideo.ts` continues to publish deprecated structure-video fields in shared task param interfaces.
`taskIdHelpers.ts` still encodes historical field fallback (`source_task_id`, `orchestrator_task_id`, `task_id`), indicating canonical task-id contracts are not yet singular.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical v2 task payload contract and isolate all legacy parsing into a boundary translator module with hard sunset gates; then remove legacy fields from shared interfaces so new call sites cannot compile against deprecated keys.

findings.summary: Confirm handler can leave stale pending delete ID when project is missing
evidence: In `handleConfirm`, when `pendingDeleteId` exists but `selectedProjectId` is falsy, function returns early without clearing `pendingDeleteId`.
Dialog `open` state is derived from `pendingDeleteId !== null`, so this early return can leave confirmation UI state stale/open until manually dismissed.
findings.suggestion: Clear `pendingDeleteId` before returning on missing `selectedProjectId` (and optionally surface an error toast) to keep dialog state transitions deterministic.

findings.summary: Drag-drop utilities are split across three top-level files, but two split files are unused
evidence: src/shared/lib/dragDrop.ts contains both DataTransfer detection and preview rendering logic (hasDataTransferType and createDragPreview).
src/shared/lib/dragDropCore.ts and src/shared/lib/dragDropPreview.ts contain near-identical extracted logic, but there are no import references to either file in src/.
review_context.existing_findings already reports dragDropCore.ts as orphaned (0 importers) and dragDropPreview.ts as untested (0 importers), so the split is not active architecture.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Target folder: create src/shared/lib/dragDrop/ and choose one canonical shape (either keep monolith as index.ts or adopt core.ts + preview.ts + index.ts facade). 2) Move order: first move dragDrop.ts to src/shared/lib/dragDrop/index.ts, then either delete dragDropCore.ts and dragDropPreview.ts if not adopting split, or move their implementations into the new folder and remove duplicates from index.ts. 3) Import updates/validation: run `rg "@/shared/lib/dragDrop" src -n` to update all callers to the chosen entrypoint, then run `rg "dragDropCore|dragDropPreview" src -n` to confirm no stale paths, and finish with `pnpm vitest src/shared/lib/__tests__/dragDrop.test.ts` (or project test command) plus `pnpm tsc --noEmit`.

findings.summary: The top-level shared hooks directory is overloaded and mixes stable hooks with temporary aliases
evidence: holistic_context.structure.directory_profiles reports src/shared/hooks/ file_count = 81, indicating a flat high-volume package with mixed responsibilities.
src/shared/hooks/useCredits.ts is explicitly a deprecation shim forwarding to feature billing hooks, while useDarkMode/useDebounce are core utility hooks in the same folder.
The colocated shared-hooks tests include module-level tests for both core hooks and alias-driven paths, reinforcing a catch-all package shape instead of clear subpackage boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Target folders: introduce src/shared/hooks/core/ for stable generic hooks and src/shared/hooks/compat/ for temporary migration aliases. 2) Move order: move useDebounce.ts and useDarkMode.ts (+ tests) into core first, then move useCredits.ts to compat and keep a thin re-export at the old path only during migration window. 3) Import updates/validation: run `rg "@/shared/hooks/use(Debounce|DarkMode|Credits)" src -n` and rewrite imports to new locations, then run `pnpm tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebounce.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDarkMode.test.ts src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebouncedSettingsSave.test.ts`.

findings.summary: Legacy compatibility hooks in shared space weaken clear domain ownership
evidence: useCredits.ts documents itself as a compatibility shim with removal target 2026-06-30, but remains a public shared hook entrypoint.
The test suite continues exercising shared-hook access patterns for settings-save behavior, preserving the shared surface as a long-term dependency path.
This pattern makes it harder to infer whether `src/shared/hooks` is a stable domain-neutral API or a temporary migration layer.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) Target folders: centralize temporary APIs under src/shared/compat/hooks/ and keep domain-owned hooks under feature/settings packages only. 2) Move order: first relocate useCredits shim into compat folder with explicit expiry registry, then update tests to import domain-owned hooks directly (keeping only one contract test for compat path). 3) Import updates/validation: run `rg "@/shared/hooks/useCredits|@/shared/hooks/useDebouncedSettingsSave" src -n` to migrate callers, then run `pnpm tsc --noEmit`, `pnpm vitest src/shared/hooks/__tests__/useDebouncedSettingsSave.test.ts`, and a full test pass for billing/settings hook suites.

findings.summary: Supabase client keeps a legacy lazy-init fallback that weakens the bootstrap contract.
evidence: `getSupabaseClient()` catches runtime access failure, logs via `handleError`, warns only in DEV, then calls `initializeSupabase()` as a fallback path.
README describes bootstrap initialization as a contract, but runtime behavior still supports out-of-order access through recovery.
findings.suggestion: Harden the contract in stages: (1) keep `initializeSupabase()` as the only startup path and make `getSupabaseClient()` fail-fast with a typed bootstrap error in production/dev; (2) move fallback behavior behind an explicit legacy facade module only; (3) update callers to initialize at bootstrap and remove implicit recovery branch; validate with `npm run check:contracts`, `npm run check:legacy-supabase-usage`, and targeted runtime tests for uninitialized access.

findings.summary: README governance contract references deprecated exports that no longer exist.
evidence: README section 'Error Handling Deprecations' lists `reportRuntimeError` and `RuntimeErrorOptions` as deprecated aliases in `handleError.ts`.
Current `handleError.ts` exports are `reportError`, `reportAndRethrow`, `handleError`, and `handleAndRethrow`; the listed deprecated aliases are absent.
findings.suggestion: Update README governance text to match runtime reality: remove stale alias references, document the current public contract in `handleError.ts`, and keep only active migration gates. Add a lightweight docs-contract check step in CI (for example a script asserting documented symbols exist) so governance docs cannot drift again.

findings.summary: Canonical generation/shot type barrel lives in an overloaded root `src/types` package.
evidence: `generationAndShots.ts` is labeled canonical and has very high importer count, but resides in generic `src/types/` rather than a domain-scoped package.
Holistic structure shows `src/types/` has 12 files with mixed concerns and high average fan-in (22.1), indicating root-level centralization rather than domain ownership.
README positions `@/types/generationAndShots` as canonical compatibility surface, reinforcing the root-hub dependency pattern.
findings.suggestion: Run a staged reorg: 1) create `src/domains/generation/types/public.ts` (or `index.ts`) as canonical domain entrypoint; 2) move generation/shot type files from `src/types/` into `src/domains/generation/types/` in this order: `generationParams.ts`, `generationMetadata.ts`, `generationRow.ts`, `shot.ts`, then the barrel; 3) keep `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` as temporary shim re-export with deprecation marker; 4) migrate imports with `rg "@/types/generationAndShots|@/types/(generation|shot)" -n src` and update paths; 5) validate with `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test -- src/types/__tests__/generationAndShots.contract.test.ts`, and `npm run check:contracts`; 6) remove shim after budget reaches zero.

findings.summary: Lightbox split introduced contract fanout with high pass-through indirection instead of clearer ownership boundaries.
evidence: `MediaLightboxProps` is very broad (lines 11-77), then `useBundledLightboxProps` re-groups mostly unchanged fields (lines 80-147) before passing them down.
`ImageLightbox` unpacks those grouped props and forwards a large mapped object into `useSharedLightboxState` (lines 228-278), creating a map-unmap flow instead of reducing interface size.
`useSharedLightboxState` itself accepts a composed multi-interface prop object and returns a large nested facade (lines 126-252, 626-730), concentrating many concerns behind one hook boundary.
findings.suggestion: Define one typed lightbox view-model per mode (`image`, `video`, `segment-slot`) and construct it in a dedicated adapter layer. Remove map-unmap passes by letting `ImageLightbox`/`VideoLightbox` consume only mode-specific inputs and splitting `useSharedLightboxState` into narrower hooks grouped by domain (navigation, variants, shot actions, references).

findings.summary: Timeline orchestration is a god hook with too many responsibilities and an oversized public surface.
evidence: `UseTimelineOrchestratorReturn` exposes a very large mixed surface (refs, zoom, drag, selection, drop, computed data, local UI state) in one contract (lines 54-139).

Implementation handles many distinct workflows in one hook: drag freezing, zoom centering, global events, drop interceptors, duplicate collision search, structure video placement, endpoint drag, computed rendering data (lines 163-569).

`Timeline.tsx` already acts as another orchestrator for domain loading, lightbox, derived navigation, adapters, and cache lookups (lines 101-300), so the orchestration burden is split across two large controllers rather than sharply partitioned.
findings.suggestion: Split `useTimelineOrchestrator` into mode-specific controllers (`interactionController`, `dropController`, `structureVideoController`, `viewportController`) and expose a thin composed API consumed by `TimelineContainer`. Keep collision logic and placement policies in pure domain utilities with independent tests.

findings.summary: Form submission flow uses low-leverage wrapper layers plus duplicated task-queue execution shape.
evidence: `useFormSubmission` forwards a large prop set into `useSubmissionHandlers` with little behavior beyond ref snapshot and builder wiring (lines 20-109).
`useSubmissionHandlers` mostly wires commands between helper hooks (lines 25-88), while `usePromptQueueHandlers` is nearly pure callback forwarding (lines 18-37).

`useSubmissionOrchestrator` repeats similar queue task envelopes in `submitManaged`, `queueExisting`, and `queueLikeExisting` (same label/expectedCount/toastTitle/execute shape across lines 91-101, 193-203, 224-257), increasing branch duplication.
findings.suggestion: Collapse thin forwarding hooks into a single coordinator with explicit state machine events (`submitManaged`, `submitAutomated`, `queueExisting`, `queueLikeExisting`) and extract a reusable `runQueuedGenerationTask` helper for shared queue envelope construction.

findings.summary: Multiple task-param schema variants leak into runtime logic, causing repeated fallback branches across modules.
evidence: `params.ts` encodes many path fallbacks per field (`run_id`, `based_on`, `shot_id`, `add_in_position`) via path arrays and generic traversal (lines 42-163), signaling polymorphic payload shapes.

`generation-child.ts` still manually re-implements nested fallback lookups for fields like `toolType` and `generationName` (`full_orchestrator_payload`/`orchestrator_details`/top-level checks, lines 152-156 and 254-256), bypassing the extractor abstraction.

`index.ts` mutates task params in-flight (`setThumbnailInParams`, shot cleanup, then overwrite `taskContext.params`, lines 286-309), so normalization and business operations are interleaved rather than isolated.
findings.suggestion: Add one canonical `NormalizedTaskParams` builder at the start of completion flow and require downstream modules (`generation-child`, orchestrator checks, storage metadata updates) to consume only that typed normalized object. Move all legacy-path fallbacks into that single adapter layer.
